project: ACIM Daily Minute — iOS / macOS / watchOS / Widget
mission: Post-ship bug sprint II (2026-04-15 evening) targeting seven user-visible defects Larry identified by dropping 8 screenshots into /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/untracked/ after testing the iPad-sim build that came out of sprint I (ACIM-201 → ACIM-208, commits 409cd6d through 498db6c). Five of sprint I's eight stories were marked passes:true but the new screenshots prove the UI is still broken: Listen → Minute shows 1 row while the feed has 25 items, Listen → Lessons shows 1 row while the feed has 12 items, the Today view body text still doesn't flow full width on iPad, the mini-player still covers bottom rows of the Lessons spine, LessonDetailView for Lesson 1 still shows the pre-ACIM-208 'not yet cached' placeholder + truncated RSS title + Refresh button, and the bundled ACIMChime.caf in Resources (507148 bytes, Apr 13) is a stale predecessor of the intended 714842-byte Apr 14 chime in /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/assets/ACIMChime.caf. Two user decisions captured in planning: (1) Bug #6 is fixed by bundling full lesson-body text for all 365 workbook lessons — Larry personally drops /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/ACIMDailyMinute/Resources/Workbook365Bodies.json with schema [{"lessonNumber": Int, "body": String}] and the story codes the reader + view wiring around it; when the file is absent or a lesson is missing from it, LessonDetailView falls back to the ACIM Daily Lesson YouTube playlist embed at index = lessonNumber (ACIM-208 pattern). (2) Bug #7 is fixed by a binary replace of /ACIMDailyMinute/Resources/ACIMChime.caf with the bytes of /assets/ACIMChime.caf — no pbxproj changes needed (membership already correct per sprint I's ACIM-202 diagnosis), no code changes in NotificationManager. Eight screenshots map to seven stories because the two Listen → Minute screenshots are the same symptom captured hours apart (one story), and the Listen → Lessons list-sparse screenshot plus the YouTube-mismatch screenshot are root-and-consequence of the same pipeline bug (one story). All work in this PRD lands directly on main — no branches, no backwards-compat shims, no dead code, no TODOs. Each story = one Ralph iteration = one ./bu.sh commit straight to origin/main. ACIM-307 is a dedicated end-to-end verification story whose existence is the countermeasure to sprint I's false passes:true pattern — Ralph may not emit <promise>COMPLETE</promise> until every ACIM-301 through ACIM-306 acceptance check re-passes on a fresh simulator install.

executionModel.ralphRunsOn: M4 / current machine. Working directory: /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp. Ralph has full bash + Xcode toolchain. No Python or external services required.
executionModel.buildTarget: iPad (10th generation) / iOS 18.1, simulator UUID 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28. Use ./build.sh which resolves the iPad sim and the Apple Watch Series 10 (46mm) sim. Watch + Widget targets compile clean as of sprint I's final commit 498db6c.
executionModel.commitCommand: ./bu.sh "<id> — <chunk title>" — runs git add -A + commit + push origin main + Dropbox zip backup. bu.sh's hard-coded `git push origin main` is correct for this PRD (we are on main). No branch fix-up needed.
executionModel.verificationCommand: ./build.sh > /tmp/full-build.log 2>&1; echo exit=$? — builds all 3 targets (main app + Widget + Watch). Every story in this PRD MUST produce ./build.sh exit code 0.
executionModel.errorFilter: grep 'error:' /tmp/full-build.log | wc -l — must equal 0 for every story. If non-zero, the story is incomplete; do not commit, fix the errors first.
executionModel.alreadyShipped: Sprint I (ACIM-201 → ACIM-208) claimed passes:true for all 8 stories but evidence shows ACIM-202 (notification sound), ACIM-204 (Lessons list bottom inset), ACIM-205 (Today view text flow + bottom inset), ACIM-206 (Listen Minute list all-items), ACIM-207 (Listen Lessons list all-items), and ACIM-208 (LessonDetailView YouTube fallback) are broken on the current iPad-sim build. Sprint II re-ships those six broken surfaces correctly + adds the new decision to bundle lesson bodies. ACIM-201 (Settings ACIM Sparkly tagline + strip Sparkly refs from Onboarding/About) and ACIM-203 (Archive Today button) remain shipped-and-working; do NOT regress those during sprint II.
executionModel.untrackedScreenshots: /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/untracked/ holds the 8 source screenshots for this sprint (see screenshotToStoryMap below). Ralph MUST NOT delete, rename, or modify any file in that directory. It is Larry's evidence folder.

rules.onePerIteration: Implement exactly one story per Ralph iteration, lowest priority number first that still has passes:false AND whose depends_on stories all have passes:true.
rules.buildPolicy: Each story MUST run the verificationCommand from executionModel before committing. ./build.sh MUST exit 0. Run the errorFilter as a secondary sanity check. Story is incomplete if either gate fails — do NOT commit and do NOT set passes:true.
rules.commitFormat: ./bu.sh "<chunk-id> — <chunk title>" — exact format. Example: "ACIM-301 — Bundled ACIMChime.caf replaced with /assets/ 714KB version". One commit per story. After commit succeeds, set the story's passes:true in this prd.json AND append a story-completion block to progress.txt under '## --- Iteration Log ---'.
rules.mainOnly: All work commits directly to main. Do NOT create feature branches. Do NOT open PRs. Do NOT amend prior commits — every story is a fresh commit on main.
rules.noTodos: No TODO / FIXME / XXX / HACK comments anywhere in committed code. Pick a sensible default and fully implement. Verify per story: grep -rIn 'TODO\|FIXME\|XXX\|HACK' on the changed files must be empty.
rules.noDeadCode: Delete anything being replaced. No commented-out code, no unused symbols, no `_ = oldVar` re-binds, no shim functions that just call the new function. Verify per story: review the diff and confirm zero deletions are present-but-commented.
rules.noBackwardsCompat: No legacy fallbacks for the prior implementation. No deprecated-key reads, no dual-write shims, no 'old' branches kept alive. Cut clean.
rules.noJtfnewsRefs: Absolute rule — zero 'JTFNews' / 'jtfnews' / 'JTF News' mentions in any committed file (code, comments, docs, plist, pbxproj). The pattern library at /Users/larryseyer/jtfnewsapp/ is read-only; never name it inside this repo. Verify per story: grep -rIn 'JTFNews\|jtfnews\|JTF News' on the changed files must be empty.
rules.scopeFence: No refactors beyond what each story requires. No feature additions. Don't touch shipped surfaces other than the specific files each story names. Do NOT regress ACIM-201 (Settings tagline / Onboarding / About) or ACIM-203 (Archive Today button).
rules.untrackedFolderReadOnly: Files in /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/untracked/ are evidence artifacts. Ralph MUST NOT delete, move, rename, or edit them. Read-only reference only.
rules.verificationHonesty: Sprint I's failure mode was setting passes:true without actually verifying the acceptance criteria on the iPad sim. For sprint II, every story's iPad-sim walkthrough check MUST be performed before passes:true is set. If Ralph cannot boot the simulator or run the walkthrough within the iteration, set passes remains false and append a 'blocked: <reason>' line to the story block in progress.txt. ACIM-307 is the explicit end-to-end re-verification gate; passes:true on 301-306 individually is not enough — 307 must also pass before <promise>COMPLETE</promise>.
rules.completionSignal: After story ACIM-307 commits AND ./build.sh exits 0, output the literal string <promise>COMPLETE</promise> on its own line so run_ralph.sh exits cleanly.

stories.title: Bundled ACIMChime.caf replaced with /assets/ 714KB version so test notification plays correct chime
stories.why: Screenshot 'Wrong notification sound plays - (use :Users:larryseyer:ACIMDailyMinuteApp:assets).png' shows Settings → Send test notification producing a banner ('ACIM Daily Minute — Test notification — your daily reminder is configured.') but Larry reports the audible chime is wrong. Investigation: /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/ACIMDailyMinute/Resources/ACIMChime.caf is 507148 bytes (Apr 13 timestamp) while /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/assets/ACIMChime.caf is 714842 bytes (Apr 14 timestamp). The filename hint '(use :Users:larryseyer:ACIMDailyMinuteApp:assets)' confirms Larry wants the newer /assets/ copy shipped in the bundle. NotificationManager.preferredSound() already correctly references `UNNotificationSound(named: UNNotificationSoundName("ACIMChime.caf"))` per sprint I's ACIM-202 diagnosis (commit b7a523c) — the bug is the bundled bytes, not the Swift code or the pbxproj membership. Fix is a pure binary replace.
acceptanceCriteria: Binary-replace /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/ACIMDailyMinute/Resources/ACIMChime.caf with the contents of /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/assets/ACIMChime.caf: `cp -f /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/assets/ACIMChime.caf /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/ACIMDailyMinute/Resources/ACIMChime.caf`. After the copy, `stat -f '%z' /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/ACIMDailyMinute/Resources/ACIMChime.caf` MUST return 714842 (exact byte match to the /assets/ source). `md5 -q` of both files MUST match.
Do NOT modify NotificationManager.swift in this story. Do NOT modify project.pbxproj in this story. Do NOT add or remove any other file. The diff is exactly one binary file content change.
Run verification: ./build.sh > /tmp/full-build.log 2>&1. ./build.sh MUST exit 0. grep 'error:' /tmp/full-build.log | wc -l MUST equal 0.

Post-build bundle verification: `find /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/build -name 'ACIMChime.caf' -path '*ACIMDailyMinute.app*' -exec stat -f '%z' {} \;` MUST output 714842 for every matching path (iOS sim, iOS device, macOS bundle). No path returning 507148.

Verify on iPad sim 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28 (simulator audio unmuted on host Mac): launch app → Settings → Send test notification → banner appears AND the 714KB chime plays audibly through the host Mac speakers. The new chime is longer/fuller than the 507KB predecessor — confirm by ear it is the intended sound.

grep -rIn 'TODO\|FIXME\|XXX\|HACK\|JTFNews\|jtfnews\|JTF News' /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/ACIMDailyMinute/Resources/ACIMChime.caf returns nothing (binary file, trivially clean — just run the check to keep the per-story gate consistent).
Commit: ./bu.sh "ACIM-301 — Bundled ACIMChime.caf replaced with /assets/ 714KB version". Mark passes:true in prd.json and append progress log.

stories.title: Today view body text flows full iPad width minus 20pt horizontal padding
stories.why: Screenshot 'Lesson text does not flow - why right side blank.png' shows the Today tab on iPad with the lesson body text (both the Daily Minute body AND the Lesson 11 body inside the Workbook section) rendering in what appears to be phone-width (~375pt), leaving a large blank region on the right half of the iPad screen. Sprint I's ACIM-205 (commit e38f916) claimed to apply `.frame(maxWidth: .infinity, alignment: .leading)` on both DailyMinuteCard and DailyLessonCard body Text views, but the current screenshot proves either the modifier was removed, or a parent container is capping width above the Text views, or the modifier was never reached at the render tree level. Investigation must find the actual cap (likely a nested VStack/ViewThatFits/GeometryReader/.frame(maxWidth: 720)) and remove or widen it. Outer card padding stays at 20pt horizontal — the iPad wants 'full width minus standard 20pt side padding,' not a centered reading column.
acceptanceCriteria: Read TodayView.swift, DailyMinuteCard.swift, and DailyLessonCard.swift top-to-bottom. Identify every explicit width cap in the render tree between the outer ScrollView/List and the body Text views. Width caps include: `.frame(maxWidth: <value>)` with a finite value, `.frame(width: <value>)`, `GeometryReader { proxy in ... proxy.size.width * <fraction> }`, `ViewThatFits` with width-limited variants, container modifiers like `.containerRelativeFrame(.horizontal)` with a fraction < 1. Remove or widen every such cap on the path from root to body Text.

DailyMinuteCard.swift body Text: ensure the chain includes `.frame(maxWidth: .infinity, alignment: .leading)` and `.fixedSize(horizontal: false, vertical: true)` directly on the Text view. If those modifiers are already present but width is still capped, the cap is on an ancestor — fix the ancestor, do not add duplicate modifiers.

DailyLessonCard.swift body Text: same treatment as DailyMinuteCard — `.frame(maxWidth: .infinity, alignment: .leading)` + `.fixedSize(horizontal: false, vertical: true)` on the body Text. Title Text retains its existing alignment (do not touch).

TodayView.swift: the outer VStack/ScrollView that holds DailyMinuteCard and DailyLessonCard MUST NOT apply `.frame(maxWidth: <finite value>)` or any `.padding(.horizontal, <value>)` greater than 20pt. The only horizontal padding allowed on the card containers is `.padding(.horizontal, 20)` — use exactly 20pt, no readable-column caps, no dynamic type-based widening.
Do NOT add any new maxWidth caps, GeometryReader wrappers, or ViewThatFits blocks. Do NOT introduce a centered reading column.
Run verification: ./build.sh > /tmp/full-build.log 2>&1. ./build.sh MUST exit 0. grep 'error:' /tmp/full-build.log | wc -l MUST equal 0.

Verify on iPad sim 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28 in landscape orientation: open Today tab → both the Daily Minute body Text and the Daily Lesson body Text span the full iPad width minus 20pt on each side. No large blank region on the right. Take a screenshot (`xcrun simctl io booted screenshot /tmp/acim-302-verify.png`) and visually confirm body-text right-edge is within 25pt of the safe-area right edge (20pt padding + ~5pt tolerance).
Verify in portrait orientation too — same behavior, just narrower. No regression of card visual styling (colors, corner radius, shadow stay as they were pre-fix).
grep -rIn 'TODO\|FIXME\|XXX\|HACK\|JTFNews\|jtfnews\|JTF News' on the three touched files must be empty.
Commit: ./bu.sh "ACIM-302 — Today view body text flows full iPad width minus 20pt padding". Mark passes:true in prd.json and append progress log.

stories.title: Mini-player bottom safe-area inset on every root scrollable view (Lessons + Today + LessonDetail + Listen Minute + Listen Lessons)
stories.why: Screenshot 'Player at bottom covers up lesson titles.png' shows the Lessons spine view with rows 17 and 18 (and implicitly any row deeper in the 365-item list) partially covered by the 'Daily Minute' mini-player docked at the bottom of the screen. Sprint I's ACIM-204 (commit 44641a4) claimed to add `.safeAreaInset(edge: .bottom)` to LessonsView with MiniPlayerView.height = 56, but the current screenshot proves either the inset was removed, MiniPlayerView.height is incorrect (real rendered height ≠ 56), the inset is on the wrong container, or the AudioManager.hasActiveAudio check returns false at the wrong moment. The fix applies the same inset modifier to EVERY root scrollable view, not just LessonsView, so no list anywhere in the app has its last rows covered when audio is active. Root scrollable views to cover: LessonsView (365-lesson spine), TodayView (Daily Minute + Daily Lesson cards), LessonDetailView (full-text lesson body, Absent state too), ListenView's Minute sub-tab list, ListenView's Lessons sub-tab list.
acceptanceCriteria: MiniPlayerView.swift: verify `public static let height: CGFloat = 56` is still present (added by sprint I's ACIM-204). If absent, re-add it. If present but the rendered height is NOT 56pt (measure by reading the outer VStack/HStack padding + content + stroke), update the constant to match the actual rendered height. Never hard-code a different number elsewhere — every bottom-inset site uses MiniPlayerView.height.

LessonsView.swift: the outermost scrollable container (the List or ScrollView that holds the 365-row spine and the release-cadence header) MUST have `.safeAreaInset(edge: .bottom, spacing: 0) { Color.clear.frame(height: audio.hasActiveAudio ? MiniPlayerView.height : 0) }`. AudioManager is injected via `@Environment(AudioManager.self) private var audio` (reuse existing injection, do not create a new instance). If the inset was removed from LessonsView since sprint I, restore it. If it's present on the wrong container (e.g., on a child ForEach or on the header), move it to the outermost container.

TodayView.swift: apply the identical safeAreaInset modifier to its outermost ScrollView/List that contains DailyMinuteCard + DailyLessonCard. Same Color.clear frame, same conditional audio.hasActiveAudio ? MiniPlayerView.height : 0, same AudioManager injection.

LessonDetailView.swift: apply the identical safeAreaInset modifier to ITS outermost ScrollView, so when the Full state renders a long body Text (or the Absent state renders a YouTube embed per ACIM-304), the bottom portion is never covered by the mini-player.

ListenView.swift: apply the identical safeAreaInset modifier to BOTH the Minute sub-tab list AND the Lessons sub-tab list (whichever container wraps the respective List/ScrollView). If both sub-tabs share a single outer ScrollView, apply the modifier once on that shared container.

Do NOT introduce any `.padding(.bottom, 56)` hardcodes, ever. Do NOT apply the inset to individual row views (LessonRow, MinuteRow, etc.) — the inset belongs to the scroll container. Do NOT wrap the containers in new GeometryReader to measure — MiniPlayerView.height is the single source of truth.

Audio environment injection sanity check: `grep -rIn '@Environment(AudioManager.self)' /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/ACIMDailyMinute/Views/` MUST include all five touched views (LessonsView, TodayView, LessonDetailView, ListenView, and implicitly MiniPlayerView). If a touched view is missing the environment injection, add it — do NOT create a new `@StateObject var audio = AudioManager.shared` or any other parallel instance.
Run verification: ./build.sh > /tmp/full-build.log 2>&1. ./build.sh MUST exit 0. grep 'error:' /tmp/full-build.log | wc -l MUST equal 0.

Verify on iPad sim 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28 — the walkthrough MUST exercise every touched view with audio active: (a) open Listen → Minute → tap any episode → audio starts → mini-player appears at bottom → switch to Lessons tab → scroll to the last visible row → bottom row fully visible above mini-player, no clipping. (b) Switch to Today tab → Daily Lesson card bottom text fully visible above mini-player. (c) Tap into a specific lesson (e.g., today's Lesson 11) → LessonDetailView Full state body bottom fully visible. (d) Go back to Listen → Minute list and Lessons list → both lists' last rows fully visible. (e) Stop audio → mini-player disappears → each list extends all the way to the bottom safe-area with zero empty gap.
grep -rIn 'TODO\|FIXME\|XXX\|HACK\|JTFNews\|jtfnews\|JTF News' on the five touched files must be empty.
Commit: ./bu.sh "ACIM-303 — Mini-player bottom inset on Lessons + Today + LessonDetail + Listen Minute + Lessons". Mark passes:true in prd.json and append progress log.

stories.title: LessonDetailView bundled-body-text render with YouTube playlist fallback for missing lessons
stories.why: Screenshot 'Where is the rest of the text for this lesson?.png' shows LessonDetailView for Lesson 1 rendering the pre-ACIM-208 UI: a 'LESSON 1' tag, a date '2026-03-31', the truncated RSS-style opening quote '"Nothing I see in this room [on this street,' (no closing quote, no body), and a placeholder line 'Full text available once today's lesson fetches this entry.' plus a Listen button. Sprint I's ACIM-208 (commit 498db6c) claimed to replace this state with a YouTube playlist embed + Workbook365 canonical title, but the current screenshot proves either the fix was reverted, the code path isn't reached (wrong Absent-state routing), or the view got rebuilt against a different source-of-truth branch. Larry's decision during planning: bundle full lesson body text for all 365 lessons. Larry personally drops /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/ACIMDailyMinute/Resources/Workbook365Bodies.json with schema `[{"lessonNumber": Int, "body": String}]`. This story codes a WorkbookBodiesCatalog service that reads that file lazily, plus a rewrite of LessonDetailView's Absent state to render bundled body when available and fall back to the YouTube playlist embed (ACIM-208 pattern) when the file or a specific lesson entry is missing.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/ACIMDailyMinute/Services/WorkbookBodiesCatalog.swift — a new Swift file declaring `public enum WorkbookBodiesCatalog` with three static members: (1) a private static `let entries: [Int: String]` lazily computed by reading Bundle.main.url(forResource: "Workbook365Bodies", withExtension: "json"), JSONDecoding into `[Entry]` where `struct Entry: Decodable { let lessonNumber: Int; let body: String }`, and reducing into a dictionary keyed by lessonNumber. If the file is absent from the bundle (URL is nil) OR JSONDecoding throws, entries is an empty dictionary — no print, no assertionFailure, no throw up the stack. (2) `public static func body(for lessonNumber: Int) -> String?` which returns `entries[lessonNumber]`. (3) `public static var isEmpty: Bool { entries.isEmpty }` for test/diagnostic use but NOT referenced from the UI. The file is <40 lines total.

Register WorkbookBodiesCatalog.swift in project.pbxproj as a new file on the iOS app target: add a PBXFileReference under the Services group, add a PBXBuildFile referencing it, add the PBXBuildFile ID to the iOS app target's PBXSourcesBuildPhase files[] array. Mirror the pattern used for other Services/*.swift files — find one (e.g., Services/AudioManager.swift or Services/PodcastService.swift) and copy the exact pbxproj shape.

Register Workbook365Bodies.json in project.pbxproj on the iOS app target's Copy Bundle Resources phase so the build graph knows about it and copies it into the .app bundle if present. Add PBXFileReference with path `Workbook365Bodies.json`, sourceTree `<group>`, parent the Resources group (same group as Workbook365.json). Add PBXBuildFile. Add the PBXBuildFile ID to the iOS app target's PBXResourcesBuildPhase files[] array. Mirror the exact shape of Workbook365.json's pbxproj entries. If Workbook365Bodies.json does not yet exist at commit time, Xcode will still build — Xcode's Copy Files phase silently skips missing files referenced by PBXFileReference. Do NOT add a Run Script phase or any other mechanism to fabricate the file.

LessonDetailView.swift: locate the Absent state view (the branch rendered when the lesson is NOT in the DailyLesson/ArchivedReading SwiftData cache — currently named something like `AbsentLessonView` or rendered inline via `if let cachedLesson = ... else { ... }`). Delete ALL of the old Absent-state UI: the ContentUnavailableView with book icon, the 'Lesson N not yet cached' text, the explanatory paragraph ('Open this lesson when it's the Daily Lesson, or tap Refresh...'), the Refresh button, the refresh() closure/async method body. Replace the ENTIRE Absent state with the following render tree:

Absent state render tree (exact structure): ScrollView { VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 16) { Text(WorkbookCatalog.title(for: lessonNumber) ?? "Lesson \(lessonNumber)").font(.system(.title2, design: .serif).weight(.semibold)).frame(maxWidth: .infinity, alignment: .leading); if let body = WorkbookBodiesCatalog.body(for: lessonNumber) { Text(body).font(.body).frame(maxWidth: .infinity, alignment: .leading).fixedSize(horizontal: false, vertical: true) } else { YouTubePlayerView(playlistID: YouTubePlaylists.dailyLesson, startIndex: lessonNumber).aspectRatio(16.0/9.0, contentMode: .fit) } }.padding(.horizontal, 20).padding(.vertical, 16) }.safeAreaInset(edge: .bottom, spacing: 0) { Color.clear.frame(height: audio.hasActiveAudio ? MiniPlayerView.height : 0) }. The heading Text comes first, then either bundled body OR YouTube embed, then nothing else.

Introduction guard (lessonNumber == 0): LessonDetailView's Absent-state branch MUST NOT call WorkbookBodiesCatalog.body(for: 0) (catalog starts at 1) or YouTubePlayerView at index=0 (playlist is 1-indexed). If lessonNumber == 0 and no cached DailyLesson exists, fall back to rendering the audio-player row with the podcast feed's `audio/daily-lessons/lesson-000.mp3` URL (reuse the existing audio-row component used in the Full state's Listen button). Keep this branch small — an `if lessonNumber == 0 { IntroductionAudioFallback() }` guard at the top of the Absent state branch.

Delete all references to `refresh()` / `isRefreshing` / any Refresh-specific state from LessonDetailView. If those symbols are declared but now unused, remove the declarations. grep `func refresh` and grep `Refresh` on LessonDetailView.swift must each return 0 matches after the edit (EXCEPTION: if `Refresh` appears for a different purpose in the Full state — e.g., pull-to-refresh on the ScrollView — keep that; document in progress.txt that the remaining match is scoped to Full state pull-to-refresh).
grep 'not yet cached' /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/ACIMDailyMinute/Views/Lessons/LessonDetailView.swift MUST return 0 matches (strict). The copy is gone entirely.

YouTubePlaylists.swift (existing file shipped by sprint I's ACIM-208) provides `public static let dailyLesson: String`. YouTubePlayerView.swift (existing) accepts playlistID + startIndex parameters. If either file has regressed or changed signature since 498db6c, restore the signatures first — this story depends on both being callable as described.

Run verification: ./build.sh > /tmp/full-build.log 2>&1. ./build.sh MUST exit 0. grep 'error:' /tmp/full-build.log | wc -l MUST equal 0. Build MUST succeed WITHOUT Workbook365Bodies.json present — the Copy Bundle Resources step for a referenced-but-missing file is a warning, not an error, in Xcode (verify by reading the build log: grep -i 'warning.*Workbook365Bodies' /tmp/full-build.log is allowed to match; grep -i 'error.*Workbook365Bodies' MUST not match).

Verify on iPad sim 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28 — two-phase walkthrough: Phase A (Workbook365Bodies.json absent): open Lessons tab → tap Lesson 1 (not today's lesson, so not cached) → LessonDetailView Absent state renders heading 'Nothing I see in this room means anything.' (canonical WorkbookCatalog title) + 16:9 YouTube playlist embed loading the ACIM Daily Lesson playlist at index=1 (Lesson 1's video). NO 'not yet cached' text, NO Refresh button, NO truncated '[on this street,' snippet. Phase B (after Larry drops Workbook365Bodies.json into Resources/): rebuild with ./build.sh, reinstall on sim, tap Lesson 1 → heading unchanged, YouTube embed replaced by bundled body Text flowing full-width minus 20pt. Walking through Lesson 2, Lesson 3, etc. shows different bundled bodies. For lessons beyond what Workbook365Bodies.json contains (if any are missing), YouTube embed still appears as graceful fallback.

Verify lesson 0 (Introduction) path: open Lessons tab → tap Introduction row → LessonDetailView shows IntroductionAudioFallback (or equivalent audio-row) with lesson-000.mp3 audio — NO YouTube embed, NO bundled-body lookup. Tapping play starts the audio.

Verify no regression on Full state (lesson IS cached): tap today's lesson (Lesson 11 on 2026-04-15) → LessonDetailView shows the full body text from DailyLesson cache as before. No YouTube embed here. No bundled-body lookup here — Full state is unchanged by this story except for the ACIM-303 safeAreaInset (already applied in prior story).
grep -rIn 'TODO\|FIXME\|XXX\|HACK\|JTFNews\|jtfnews\|JTF News' on the three touched files must be empty.
Commit: ./bu.sh "ACIM-304 — LessonDetailView bundled body + YouTube playlist fallback". Mark passes:true in prd.json and append progress log.

stories.title: Listen Minute sub-tab list renders all 25 cached Daily Minute episodes descending by publishedAt
stories.why: Two screenshots — 'Bottom list of daily minute links should contain all previous - sort decending from today or latest daily minute.png' (7:47 AM) and 'We should see April 14, 13, 12 11, 10 etc below as links.png' (6:52 PM) — show the Listen tab's Minute sub-tab with only ONE row below the YouTube embed: 'Daily Minute — April 14, 2026'. `curl -s https://www.acimdailyminute.org/podcast-minute.xml | grep -c '<item>'` returns 25, so the server is emitting 25 episodes. Sprint I's ACIM-206 (commit e1ede85) concluded 'No code changes required. App code was already correct' — that conclusion is falsified by the current screenshots. Something in the app — fetch, parse, persist, or query — is only delivering 1 row to the view. Fix requires diagnosing which of those four pipeline stages is broken and applying a scoped fix.
acceptanceCriteria: Diagnostic step 1 (server baseline): run `curl -s https://www.acimdailyminute.org/podcast-minute.xml | grep -c '<item>'` from the Ralph shell and record the count in progress.txt under this story's diagnosis block. Expected 25 (subject to feed-time drift ±2). If count < 5, the server regressed — set blocked:"Upstream feed returned only N items" and escalate to Larry. If count >= 5, proceed.

Diagnostic step 2 (stale cache test): identify the iOS app bundle ID by reading project.pbxproj (search for `PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER`). Run `xcrun simctl uninstall 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28 <bundle-id>` to wipe SwiftData state, then `xcrun simctl install 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28 build/Debug-iphonesimulator/ACIMDailyMinute.app` to reinstall, then `xcrun simctl launch 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28 <bundle-id>` to boot. Wait 10 seconds for fetch. Screenshot Listen → Minute sub-tab. Record row count in progress.txt. If row count is now 25, the bug was stale SwiftData cache from a sprint-I era when the feed had 1 item — add a one-time cache-clear in PodcastService (see step 5a). If row count is still 1, proceed to step 3.

Diagnostic step 3 (parser gate): add a temporary `print("[ACIM-305] Parsed \(episodes.count) minute items from XML")` in PodcastService.fetchMinuteEpisodes() or wherever PodcastXMLParser's result is returned, immediately before persist() is called. Run the app, capture `xcrun simctl spawn 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28 log stream --level debug | grep ACIM-305` output for 15 seconds. Record the parsed count in progress.txt. If parsed count < curl count, bug is in PodcastXMLParser — fix per step 5b. If parsed count == curl count, proceed to step 4.

Diagnostic step 4 (persist/query gate): add a temporary `print("[ACIM-305] Query returned \(cachedMinutes.count) rows")` inside ListenView's body (right where the Minute list's data source is computed). Run the app. If query count < parsed count, bug is either in persist() (unique-constraint collision dropping rows silently) or in the @Query's SortDescriptor / Predicate. Fix per step 5c or 5d.

Fix step 5a (stale cache — if diagnostic step 2 resolved the bug): add a one-time cache-reset migration. In PodcastService, add a `UserDefaults.standard.bool(forKey: "acim305-cache-cleared-v1")` guard. If false, delete all CachedPodcastEpisode rows before fetching, then set the key to true. This runs once per install — idempotent on subsequent launches. DO NOT add this migration unless step 2 actually proved the bug was stale cache; otherwise it's dead code.

Fix step 5b (parser — if diagnostic step 3 proved parser bug): read PodcastXMLParser end-to-end. Verify `parser(_:didStartElement:)` resets per-item state (title, enclosureURL, pubDate, guid, description) ONLY when the element is `<item>`, not when it's `<channel>` or any other element. Verify `parser(_:didEndElement:)` appends the accumulated item to the episodes array ONLY when the element is `<item>` (not `</channel>`). Verify no early return in the loop. Apply a scoped fix — do NOT rewrite the parser, only correct the reset/append logic.

Fix step 5c (persist — if diagnostic step 4 proved persist bug): read PodcastService.persist(). Verify every parsed episode is passed through upsert, not just the first. Verify upsert's unique key (likely guid or enclosureURL) is not collapsing distinct episodes into one. If the unique key is wrong (e.g., a field that's empty for most items so they collide on empty-string), change it to a field that's actually unique per item (guid is standard for RSS).

Fix step 5d (query — if diagnostic step 4 proved query bug): read ListenView.swift's @Query declaration for cachedMinutes. Verify it has no fetchLimit (or a fetchLimit >= 100). Verify the #Predicate filter, if any, is correct (e.g., `channel == "minute"`). Verify SortDescriptor is `\.publishedAt, order: .reverse` (newest first). Fix any incorrect filter/sort/limit.

Remove ALL temporary print() statements added for diagnosis after the fix lands. The final diff MUST NOT contain any print() calls that weren't already present pre-story. Verify: `grep -n 'print(' /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/ACIMDailyMinute/Views/Listen/ListenView.swift /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/ACIMDailyMinute/Services/PodcastService.swift /Users/larryseyer/ACIMDailyMinuteApp/ACIMDailyMinute/Services/FeedService.swift` compared against the git-show of the same files at sprint I's final commit 498db6c MUST have zero new lines.
Run verification: ./build.sh > /tmp/full-build.log 2>&1. ./build.sh MUST exit 0. grep 'error:' /tmp/full-build.log | wc -l MUST equal 0.

Verify on iPad sim 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28 (FRESH install via simctl uninstall + install + launch): open Listen tab → Minute sub-tab → wait up to 10 seconds for fetch → list contains at least 25 rows (equal to the curl count from step 1, within ±2 for publish-time drift). Top row is the most recent Daily Minute episode (publishedAt max). Rows are strictly descending by publishedAt. Tap any row → correct episode's audio plays. Pull-to-refresh does not duplicate or drop rows.
grep -rIn 'TODO\|FIXME\|XXX\|HACK\|JTFNews\|jtfnews\|JTF News' on the three touched files must be empty.
Commit: ./bu.sh "ACIM-305 — Listen Minute list renders all 25 cached episodes descending". Mark passes:true in prd.json and append progress log.

stories.title: Listen Lessons sub-tab list renders all 12 cached lesson episodes descending; top row matches YouTube player
stories.why: Two screenshots — 'Daily Lessons only show 1 lesson at bottom - should show all available lessong on YouTube in decending list.png' (7:47 AM) and 'Youtube link does not match the listed link.png' (6:52 PM) — show the Listen tab's Lessons sub-tab with only ONE row below the YouTube embed: 'Lesson 10: My thoughts do not mean anything.' But the YouTube embed at the top shows ACIM Lesson 11 ('My meaningless thoughts are showing me a meaningless world.'), and `curl -s https://www.acimdailyminute.org/podcast-lessons.xml | grep -c '<item>'` returns 12 (Lessons 1-11 + Introduction). The mismatch in the second screenshot is a direct consequence of the list sparseness in the first: today's Lesson 11 is missing from the list, so the most recent row visible is yesterday's Lesson 10, which doesn't match the Lesson-11 video in the embed. Fix order: after ACIM-305's pipeline diagnosis lands, the Lessons channel likely renders correctly by side-effect. Verify, then polish title-display and ordering.
acceptanceCriteria: Diagnostic step 1: run `curl -s https://www.acimdailyminute.org/podcast-lessons.xml | grep -c '<item>'` — expected 12 (subject to drift). Record in progress.txt under this story's diagnosis block.

Diagnostic step 2 (verify ACIM-305's fix propagated): FRESH install via `xcrun simctl uninstall 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28 <bundle-id>` + install + launch. Open Listen → Lessons sub-tab. Count rows. Record in progress.txt. If count >= 12, ACIM-305 fixed both channels — proceed directly to step 4 (title-display polish). If count < 12, the Lessons channel has its own bug — apply step 3.

Fix step 3 (Lessons-channel-specific fix, only if step 2 shows count < 12): mirror ACIM-305's diagnostic step 3 and step 4 scoped to the Lessons feed. The PodcastXMLParser should be channel-agnostic (fixed once in ACIM-305 covers both), so the more likely bug is in the @Query predicate or the fetch URL. Read FeedService.swift to confirm the Lessons feed URL is https://www.acimdailyminute.org/podcast-lessons.xml (note the plural 'lessons'). Read ListenView.swift to confirm the Lessons @Query predicate is `channel == "lesson"` (singular, matching the persist() value). If there's a singular/plural mismatch between URL, persist channel value, and @Query predicate, reconcile them so all three use the same channel identifier value.

Title-display polish (always apply, regardless of whether step 3 ran): in ListenView's Lessons-sub-tab ForEach, the row title Text MUST resolve via a helper that (a) parses `lessonNumber` from the RSS title via `^Lesson (\d+)` prefix, OR recognizes the literal 'Introduction' as lessonNumber 0, (b) for lessonNumber > 0, returns `WorkbookCatalog.title(for: lessonNumber) ?? episode.title`, (c) for lessonNumber == 0 (Introduction), returns `episode.title` unchanged (which is 'Introduction'), (d) for any RSS title matching neither pattern, returns `episode.title` unchanged. Implement this as a static helper `ListenView.displayTitle(for episode: CachedPodcastEpisode) -> String` — <20 lines — and call it from the Lessons ForEach row. If sprint I's ACIM-207 already added this helper and it still exists at commit 498db6c, verify it matches this spec and leave it; if its signature or semantics differ, rewrite it to match.
Ordering: the Lessons sub-tab's ForEach MUST iterate over cachedLessons sorted by publishedAt descending (newest first). Verify the @Query or the sorted() call produces this order.

Top row / player alignment: the top row of the Lessons list MUST have the same lessonNumber as the episode currently displayed in the YouTube embed above. The embed loads today's lesson (Lesson 11 on 2026-04-15). The top row's displayTitle MUST exactly equal the canonical WorkbookCatalog.title(for: 11) which is 'My meaningless thoughts are showing me a meaningless world.' If the list has Lesson 11 at top with that canonical title, the mismatch in screenshot 'Youtube link does not match the listed link.png' is resolved.

Bottom row: the bottom row of the Lessons list MUST be the Introduction entry (lessonNumber 0, publishedAt 2026-03-30, displayTitle literally 'Introduction'). Tapping it starts audio playback of lesson-000.mp3.
Run verification: ./build.sh > /tmp/full-build.log 2>&1. ./build.sh MUST exit 0. grep 'error:' /tmp/full-build.log | wc -l MUST equal 0.

Verify on iPad sim 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28 (FRESH install): open Listen → Lessons sub-tab → wait up to 10 seconds → list contains at least 12 rows (equal to curl count ±1). Top row is today's Lesson 11 with canonical title 'My meaningless thoughts are showing me a meaningless world.' Bottom row is Introduction dated 2026-03-30. The YouTube embed above plays Lesson 11 and the top row visibly matches it. Tap any row → correct lesson audio plays.
grep -rIn 'TODO\|FIXME\|XXX\|HACK\|JTFNews\|jtfnews\|JTF News' on the two touched files must be empty.
Commit: ./bu.sh "ACIM-306 — Listen Lessons list renders all 12 cached lessons descending, top row matches embed". Mark passes:true in prd.json and append progress log.

stories.title: End-to-end verification pass on fresh iPad-sim install + emit COMPLETE sentinel
stories.why: Sprint I's failure mode was setting passes:true on stories whose acceptance criteria were never actually walked through on the simulator. ACIM-307 is the explicit countermeasure: no code changes, only a disciplined re-walk of every ACIM-301 through ACIM-306 acceptance check on a FRESH simulator install, with the output appended to progress.txt as a single coherent walkthrough log. Only after all six checks re-pass does Ralph emit <promise>COMPLETE</promise> and exit the loop. If ANY re-check fails, append the failure details to progress.txt, set this story's passes:false, and escalate to Larry — do NOT reset prior stories' passes values.
acceptanceCriteria: No source-code changes in this story. No edits to .swift files. No edits to pbxproj. No edits to Resources/ binaries. The only file allowed to change is progress.txt (append-only).

Fresh-install preparation: run `xcrun simctl uninstall 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28 <bundle-id>` then `./build.sh > /tmp/full-build.log 2>&1` and confirm exit 0 / 0 errors. Then `xcrun simctl install 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28 build/Debug-iphonesimulator/ACIMDailyMinute.app` and `xcrun simctl launch 86F64729-D28D-44F7-BEB9-EF34AA7B7F28 <bundle-id>`. Wait 15 seconds for first-launch fetches. Resolve bundle-id by reading PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER from project.pbxproj.

Re-verify ACIM-301 (notification sound): Settings → Send test notification → banner appears → chime plays → confirm by ear the chime is the 714KB Apr 14 version, not the old 507KB Apr 13 version. Append a one-line pass/fail to progress.txt: 'ACIM-301 re-verified: PASS — chime audibly the 714KB variant.' or 'FAIL — chime is the old variant.'

Re-verify ACIM-302 (Today view full-width text): open Today tab in landscape → take a screenshot (`xcrun simctl io booted screenshot /tmp/acim-307-today.png`) → confirm Daily Minute body and Daily Lesson body right edges are within 25pt of the safe-area right edge. Append pass/fail line to progress.txt.

Re-verify ACIM-303 (mini-player bottom inset on every view): start audio from Listen → Minute → scroll to bottom of Lessons spine → last row visible above mini-player. Switch to Today tab → Daily Lesson card bottom visible. Tap a specific cached lesson → LessonDetailView body bottom visible. Return to Listen → Minute and Lessons lists → last rows visible. Stop audio → no empty gap at bottom of any view. Append pass/fail line.

Re-verify ACIM-304 (LessonDetailView bundled body / YouTube fallback): tap Lesson 1 → heading 'Nothing I see in this room means anything.' → either bundled body text flowing full-width (if Larry dropped Workbook365Bodies.json) OR 16:9 YouTube embed loading Lesson 1's video (if file absent). NO 'not yet cached' text, NO Refresh button. Tap Introduction → audio-row fallback, no YouTube embed. Tap today's Lesson 11 → Full state body text from cache. Append pass/fail line AND whether Workbook365Bodies.json was present at the time of verification.

Re-verify ACIM-305 (Listen Minute all 25 descending): Listen → Minute → list row count >= 25 (or within ±2 of the live curl count). Top row is most recent Daily Minute. Rows strictly descending by publishedAt. Append pass/fail line with the actual row count.

Re-verify ACIM-306 (Listen Lessons all 12 descending, top row matches embed): Listen → Lessons → list row count >= 12. Top row is today's Lesson 11 with canonical title 'My meaningless thoughts are showing me a meaningless world.' Bottom row is Introduction dated 2026-03-30. YouTube embed above plays Lesson 11 — top row visibly matches. Append pass/fail line.

Regression check — sprint I features that must still work: (a) Settings sheet bottom tagline reads 'ACIM Sparkly Edition · Teddy Poppe · CIMS lineage' exactly. (b) Settings → Replay introduction → step through onboarding → no 'Sparkly Edition' text anywhere. (c) About screen first paragraph reads 'A daily passage from A Course in Miracles paired with today's Workbook Lesson, offered freely, without interpretation.' (d) Archive tab → navigate back two months → tap 'Today' button in top-right toolbar → calendar jumps to April 2026 with today's date selected. Append pass/fail line for each of the four regression checks.

If any of the above checks fail, append a 'FAIL: ACIM-30N — <specific failure detail>' line to progress.txt, set this story's passes:false, and DO NOT emit <promise>COMPLETE</promise>. Fail mode surfaces to Larry for manual intervention.
If all 10 checks (6 new + 4 regression) pass, append a 'PASS: end-to-end verification complete' summary block to progress.txt, set this story's passes:true in prd.json.
Commit: ./bu.sh "ACIM-307 — End-to-end verification + COMPLETE". After the commit succeeds, output the literal string <promise>COMPLETE</promise> on its own line so run_ralph.sh exits cleanly.
